title: Preventing Unnecessary Re-renders of React Components using Memoization

statement.markdown: The `memoizedComponent` is a React component that should not re-render when the `name` or `age` props change. However, it is currently re-rendering unnecessarily due to a logical bug in the memoization implementation.
statement.inputFormat: The `memoizedComponent` accepts `name` and `age` as props and returns a JSX element.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a JSX element only when the `name` or `age` props change.
statement.notes: The component should only re-render when the `person` object reference changes.

constraints.description: Number of times the component is rendered
Number of times the component is re-rendered due to unnecessary props change

examples.explanation: The component should only re-render when the input changes.
The component should only re-render when the input changes.
The component should not re-render when the age changes.

editorial.title: Memoized Component Optimization
editorial.intuition: Use `useMemo` to cache the result of expensive computations.
editorial.bruteForce: Re-render the component on every prop change.
editorial.betterSolution: Use `useMemo` with a dependency array to memoize the result.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use `useCallback` to memoize the callback function.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The component should only re-render when the `person` object reference changes.
